responsibilities: Develop, test, and maintain low-level firmware in C for ARM Cortex-M based microcontrollers powering embedded products.
Design and implement bootloaders, firmware update mechanisms, and secure boot processes to ensure reliable field upgrades.
Write and optimize hardware abstraction layer (HAL) and board support package (BSP) code for new hardware platforms.
Debug firmware issues at the register level using JTAG/SWD debuggers, oscilloscopes, and logic analyzers.
Collaborate closely with hardware design teams during board bring-up to validate schematics, peripherals, and power sequencing.
Implement and tune RTOS task scheduling, interrupt handling, and memory management to meet real-time performance requirements.
Optimize firmware for power consumption and battery life in resource-constrained, low-power embedded devices.
Conduct firmware code reviews, static analysis, and unit testing to maintain high reliability and code quality standards.
Build and maintain continuous integration pipelines for automated firmware builds, flashing, and regression testing.
Document firmware architecture, interface specifications, and release notes to support cross-functional and customer-facing teams.
